data.summary: Implemented and tested deterministic lexical topological sorting with cycle detection.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/topoSort.test.ts | 20 ++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/topoSort.ts      | 37 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 57 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/topoSort.ts exports topoSort returning a valid deterministic (lexical tie-break) ordering of all nodes; throws with "cycle" in the message on a cyclic graph: topoSort is exported and implements Kahn's algorithm with the ready queue re-sorted lexically each step, yielding a deterministic lexical tie-break ordering over all nodes (both edge endpoints are registered in indegree/outgoing, and duplicate edges are deduped so indegree stays correct). When result.length !== indegree.size it throws Error("cycle detected"), whose message contains "cycle".
✓ calibration/topoSort.test.ts covers a linear chain, a diamond (a first / d last / predecessors before d), and a cycle throwing: The test file has exactly these three cases: a linear chain asserting ["a","b","c"]; a diamond asserting the full ordering ["a","b","c","d"] (a first, d last) plus explicit b-before-d and c-before-d predecessor checks; and a two-node cycle asserted to throw /cycle/i.

data.summary: Implemented and tested RFC-4180 CSV line parsing.
Implemented and tested numeric core and prerelease SemVer precedence.
Implemented and tested parseDuration with all required units, validation, whitespace, and multi-unit support.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/parseCsvLine.test.ts | 12 ++++++++++++
 calibration/parseCsvLine.ts      | 29 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 41 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/parseCsvLine.ts exports parseCsvLine handling quoted fields, escaped "" quotes, embedded commas inside quotes, empty fields, and empty input. calibration/parseCsvLine.test.ts covers plain, embedded-comma, escaped-quote, empty-middle-field, and empty-input cases.: parseCsvLine.ts exports the function with a quoted-state parser: `quoted` flag suppresses comma splitting (embedded commas), `""` lookahead emits a literal quote (escaped quotes), closing quote exits quoted mode, empty fields produced by consecutive commas, and empty input yields [""] via the final push. The test file uses test.each with exactly the five required cases — plain (a,b,c), embedded comma ("a,b",c), escaped quote ("she said ""hi"""), empty middle field (a,,c), and empty input ("" → [""]) — each asserting the correct expected array.

1 commit(s):
calibration/semverCompare.test.ts | 16 ++++++++++++++++
 calibration/semverCompare.ts      | 31 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 47 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/semverCompare.ts exports semverCompare returning -1|0|1 with correct numeric core + prerelease precedence (prerelease < release, numeric<alphanumeric, longer-outranks-shorter). calibration/semverCompare.test.ts covers core ordering, equality, prerelease-vs-release, and two prerelease-identifier cases.: semverCompare.ts exports semverCompare with return type -1|0|1. Numeric core compared per-component via BigInt (proven by 1.2.0 > 1.1.9, not string order). Prerelease precedence correct: absent prerelease outranks present (prerelease < release), identifier loop returns -1/1 when one side runs out (longer outranks shorter), mixed numeric/alphanumeric ranks numeric lower, numeric identifiers compared numerically, alphanumeric lexically. Test file covers core ordering (asc and desc), exact equality, prerelease-vs-release (1.0.0-alpha < 1.0.0), and two prerelease-identifier cases (alpha.1 vs alpha.beta for numeric<alphanumeric; alpha.1 vs alpha for longer-outranks-shorter). All criteria demonstrably satisfied by the diff.

1 commit(s):
calibration/parseDuration.test.ts | 19 +++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/parseDuration.ts      | 23 +++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 42 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/parseDuration.ts exports parseDuration returning ms; handles ms/s/m/h/d, multi-unit, case-insensitive, whitespace; throws on empty/unknown-unit/negative: Exported function returns milliseconds via a unit table covering ms/s/m/h/d. Multi-unit works via sequential matchAll with a position cursor ('1h30m' = 5,400,000). Case-insensitivity via the /gi flag plus toLowerCase() on the unit lookup. Whitespace is stripped before parsing. Empty (or whitespace-only) input throws; unknown units like '5x' leave the position cursor short of the string length and throw; negatives like '-5s' fail the \d+-only regex at position 0 and throw via the same position check.
✓ calibration/parseDuration.test.ts covers 90s, 1h30m, 500ms, 2d, case-insensitivity, and two throwing cases: test.each covers 90s=90000, 1h30m=5400000, 500ms=500, 2d=172800000, case-insensitive '1H30M', plus whitespace ' 2d 4h '; a second test.each asserts throws for '' and '5x' — exactly two throwing cases.

data.summary: Implemented and tested the capacity-bounded LRU cache.
Implemented and verified the stack-based RPN evaluator with required tests.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/lruCache.test.ts | 39 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/lruCache.ts      | 26 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 65 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/lruCache.ts exports lruCache with get/set/size, capacity-bounded, recency refreshed by BOTH get and set, in-place update doesn't grow size: lruCache is exported with get/set/size. set inserts then evicts the oldest Map key when size > capacity, so it is capacity-bounded. get refreshes recency via delete+re-insert; set refreshes recency the same way (delete before set). In-place update deletes the existing key first, so size stays constant and no spurious eviction occurs.
✓ calibration/lruCache.test.ts covers plain eviction, get-refreshes-recency eviction, and the size bound: Test 1 proves plain eviction (a evicted after c inserted at capacity 2). Test 2 proves get('a') refreshes recency so b is evicted instead. Test 3 proves size() stays at 2 after an in-place update and after a further insert, and that the updated key survives with its new value while b is evicted.

1 commit(s):
calibration/rpnEval.test.ts | 18 ++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/rpnEval.ts      | 32 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 50 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/rpnEval.ts exports rpnEval evaluating +,-,*,/ via a stack over ints/decimals; throws on unknown token, operand underflow, and leftover operands. calibration/rpnEval.test.ts covers four valid expressions and two throwing (underflow + leftover) cases.: rpnEval.ts exports rpnEval with a number[] stack handling +,-,*,/; Number()+isFinite parsing accepts ints and decimals; explicit throws for unknown token (non-operator non-numeric), operand underflow (stack.length < 2), and leftover operands (stack.length !== 1 at end). The test file has an it.each with exactly four valid expressions ('3 4 +', '3 4 + 5 *', '10 2 /', '2 3 4 * +') asserting exact results, plus two throwing cases: '3 +' (underflow) and '1 2 3 +' (leftover operands). All criteria demonstrably satisfied by the diff.
